text: I feel a curious sense of displacement, tinged with slight unease. The dimly lit corridor and shadowed glass evoke a sense of mystery, as though I am on the threshold between worlds—one mundane, one filled with ideas and experimentation. Entering the second space, the words about confusion and acceptance strike a chord: here, the ambiguity becomes a deliberate space for thought. I feel contemplative, open to the unknown, and oddly reassured by the merging of uncertainty with creativity.
